Required Other language third-party pack: Alpine.js (via CDN or npm, for reactive UI)
Plotly.js (for interactive charts in frontend, included via Plotly Python library's HTML export)
Logic Analysis: config/settings.py
Contains Django project settings (database, channels, installed apps, middleware). Imports from Django and third-party packages. Depends on nothing else in project.
config/urls.py
Root URL configuration. Imports from all app urls. Depends on app urls modules.
config/wsgi.py
WSGI setup for production. Imports from settings.
manage.py
Standard Django management script. Depends on settings.
accounts/models.py
User, StudentProfile, InstructorProfile models. Depends on Django auth models.
accounts/views.py
Account registration, login, profile views. Depends on accounts models and forms.
accounts/urls.py
URL patterns for accounts. Depends on accounts views.
accounts/forms.py
Forms for user creation, login, profile editing. Depends on accounts models.
accounts/admin.py
Admin config for accounts models. Depends on accounts models.
exams/models.py
Exam, Question, Competency, Cohort, ExamAttempt models. Depends on accounts models (StudentProfile, InstructorProfile).
exams/views.py
Exam creation, taking, submission views. Depends on exams models, gamification models (for nuggets/streak), analytics utils.
exams/urls.py
URL patterns for exams. Depends on exams views.
exams/forms.py
Forms for exam/question creation. Depends on exams models.
exams/admin.py
Admin config for exams models. Depends on exams models.
gamification/models.py
Badge, StudentBadge, RewardItem models. Depends on accounts models (StudentProfile).
gamification/views.py
Badge checking, nugget wallet, store redemption views. Depends on gamification models and accounts models.
gamification/urls.py
URL patterns for gamification. Depends on gamification views.
gamification/consumers.py
WebSocket consumer for real-time timer and nugget counter. Depends on gamification models and channels.
analytics/models.py
Aggregated analytics models (if any). Depends on exams models.
analytics/views.py
Analytics dashboard views (instructor heatmap, radar charts). Depends on analytics utils, exams models, accounts models.
analytics/urls.py
URL patterns for analytics. Depends on analytics views.
analytics/utils.py
Helper functions for calculating competency coverages, heatmap data. Depends on exams models.
audit/models.py
AuditLog model. Depends on accounts models.
audit/middleware.py
Middleware to log requests. Depends on audit models.
store/models.py
Store models (RewardItem redemption). Depends on gamification models.
store/views.py
Store browsing, redemption views. Depends on store models, gamification models.
store/urls.py
URL patterns for store. Depends on store views.
templates/
HTML templates. Depends on views and context data.
static/
Static files (JS, CSS). Used by templates.
Task list: 2. accounts/models.py, accounts/forms.py, accounts/views.py, accounts/urls.py, accounts/admin.py (user authentication and profiles)
4. exams/models.py, exams/forms.py, exams/admin.py (exam data models and admin)
7. analytics/models.py, analytics/utils.py (analytics data structures and helpers)
8. exams/views.py, exams/urls.py (exam taking and management endpoints)
9. gamification/views.py, gamification/urls.py, gamification/consumers.py (gamification APIs and WebSocket)
12. templates/ and static/ (frontend files, can be parallelized with backend)
Full API spec:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Competency-Based Exam Platform API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /api/auth/register:
    post:
      summary: Register a new user (student/instructor)
      requestBody: { email, password, role }
      responses: { 201: user data }
  /api/auth/login:
    post:
      summary: Login and get JWT token
      responses: { 200: token }
  /api/exams/:
    get:
      summary: List exams (filtered by role)
    post:
      summary: Create exam (instructor)
  /api/exams/{id}/start:
    post:
      summary: Start exam attempt for student
      responses: { 200: attempt details, first question, timer }
  /api/exams/{id}/submit-answer:
    post:
      summary: Submit answer for current question
      requestBody: { question_id, response }
      responses: { 200: { correct, nuggets_earned, streak } }
  /api/exams/{id}/next-question:
    get:
      summary: Get next question in exam
      responses: { 200: question data }
  /api/exams/{id}/submit:
    post:
      summary: Finalize exam attempt
      responses: { 200: competency breakdown, study tips }
  /api/gamification/nugget-balance:
    get:
      summary: Get current nugget balance and streak
  /api/gamification/leaderboard:
    get:
      parameters: { window_days: int }
      summary: Get leaderboard for cohort
  /api/store/items:
    get:
      summary: List reward items
  /api/store/redeem:
    post:
      requestBody: { item_id }
      summary: Redeem a reward
  /api/analytics/exam/{id}:
    get:
      summary: Get cohort performance data and heatmap
  ws://.../ws/exam/{exam_id}/:
    WebSocket:
      description: Real-time timer, nugget animations, streak updates
Shared Knowledge:
Common utilities:
- audit.middleware.AuditMiddleware: Logs all user actions to AuditLog table.
- config.settings: Contains DATABASES (PostgreSQL), CHANNEL_LAYERS (Redis), installed apps list (accounts, exams, gamification, analytics, audit, store, rest_framework, channels).
- Environment variables: SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL.
- Base templates (base_instructor.html, base_student.html) define common navigation and CSS/JS includes.
- Static files: exam_timer.js, nugget_animation.js, streak_tracker.js are loaded in student exam interface.
- All models use UUID primary keys as strings for security.
Anything UNCLEAR:
1. Institutional perks: default to digital vouchers/codes, but need confirmation.
2. Leaderboard window default: 30 days, max 365 days.
3. Audit log retention: indefinite with database archiving strategy; clarify if TTL is needed.
4. Code snippet sandbox: assume Pyodide for client-side execution, but Docker is mentioned; need decision.
5. Drag-drop question type implementation: assume use of HTML5 drag and drop with JSON storage of positions.
